steps.title: Identify the subject of the clause
steps.content:
The subject is the gerund phrase "Amplifying the voices and experiences of Black soldiers." A gerund phrase functions as a single, singular noun — it always takes a singular verb regardless of any plural words inside it.
  • Choice A — were: "Were" is a plural verb. It does not agree with the singular gerund subject "Amplifying." Subject-verb agreement violation. ✗
  • Choice B — have been: "Have been" is a plural verb form. It does not agree with the singular gerund subject. Subject-verb agreement violation. ✗
  • Choice C — are: "Are" is a plural verb. It does not agree with the singular gerund subject. Subject-verb agreement violation. ✗
  • Choice D — was: "Was" is a singular verb and correctly agrees with the singular gerund subject "Amplifying." ✓

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement to gerund subjects
steps.content: Gerund phrases are always grammatically singular, so they require a singular verb. "Were," "have been," and "are" are all plural and violate subject-verb agreement. Only "was" is singular and correctly matches the gerund subject. That is the correct answer.
